scope.summary:
Goal: Port the React BlockCollectionView (MT-262 parity) to the native egui frontend. This is a multi-mode saved-view host. A saved view is a typed LoomBlock (content_type='view_def') whose definition lives in PostgreSQL. The native widget loads the view definition, executes its query against the real Loom query backend (SQL-side), and renders one of three sub-views: Table, Kanban, or Calendar. Crucially: (1) table header sort clicks call updateBlockView + re-query on the backend, never client-side sort; (2) Kanban card drag-to-lane calls updateLoomBlock ({add_tags, remove_tags}) and re-queries — never local state mutations. Expected diff shape: one new file src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/block_collection_view.rs with BlockCollectionView, TableSubView, KanbanSubView, CalendarSubView, edits to src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/mod.rs. The native Loom surface reuses the existing WP-011 module src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs (the established Loom graph surface) and the shared command/selection bus modules src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs + src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs; backend access reuses src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs (HTTP client). AccessKit author_ids are registered via the accessibility/ module (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/).

Exact files to create or edit:
- CREATE src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/block_collection_view.rs — BlockCollectionView + three sub-view structs
- EDIT src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/mod.rs — pub mod block_collection_view; + re-export

Exact APIs / React reference:
- PRIMARY REACT REFERENCES:
  (a) app/src/components/BlockCollectionView.tsx — top-level host; getBlockView + queryBlockViewResults + updateBlockView + updateLoomBlock (handle sort and card-move).
  (b) app/src/components/LoomTableView.tsx — sortable typed column table; onSortChange fires backend re-sort, never client-side.
  (c) app/src/components/LoomKanbanView.tsx — drag-and-drop kanban; DRAG_MIME='application/x-handshake-kanban-card'; handleDrop fires real updateLoomBlock mutation.
  (d) app/src/components/LoomCalendarView.tsx — buckets blocks by date field; date_from/date_to applied server-side.
- Backend bindings:
  - GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/views/definitions/{view_block_id} — getBlockView -> BlockViewRecord {block, definition: BlockViewDefinition}
  - POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/views/definitions/{view_block_id}/results — queryBlockViewResults -> BlockViewResults {kind, blocks, groups?, total_returned}
  - PATCH /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/views/definitions/{view_block_id} — updateBlockView (persist new sort definition)
  - PATCH /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks/{block_id} — updateLoomBlock ({add_tags, remove_tags}) for Kanban lane move
  - POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/views/definitions — createBlockView (for creating new saved views; wire a 'New view' button)

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Define BlockCollectionView { workspace_id: String, view_block_id: String, definition: Option<BlockViewDefinition>, results: Option<BlockViewResults>, loading: bool, error: Option<String>, status: String, kanban_drag: Option<KanbanDragState> } where KanbanDragState = { block_id: String, from_lane_key: String }.
2. On init: call getBlockView to load definition, then call queryBlockViewResults to load results. Both calls run sequentially (definition first, then results).
3. Render a mode indicator strip at top showing the current kind (table / kanban / calendar) with a kind switcher (author_id='bcv.kind.table', 'bcv.kind.kanban', 'bcv.kind.calendar'). Kind change = call updateBlockView with new definition.kind + call queryBlockViewResults.
4. Status bar: show 'Re-sorting...' / 'Moving card...' / '' via status field (author_id='bcv.status' role=Status).

TableSubView:
5. Render columns from definition.columns (or default [title, updated]). For each column, show a header button (author_id='bcv.table.sort.{field}'). Click = call flipDirection() (same logic as React: same field toggles asc/desc, new field = asc), call updateBlockView with new definition.sort, then call queryBlockViewResults. Set status='Re-sorting...' while in flight.
6. Render rows: for each block in results.blocks, render a table row (author_id='bcv.table.row.{block_id}'). Cells show: title (block.title ?? block.original_filename ?? block.block_id), created_at, updated_at, journal_date, content_type, pinned yes/no, favorite yes/no, backlink_count, mention_count, tag_count per the React LoomTableView.cellValue() function.
7. Sort indicator: if definition.sort.field == column, show ▲ (asc) or ▼ (desc) after the column label.

KanbanSubView:
8. Render lanes from results.groups (BlockViewLane[]). Each lane has a key (author_id='bcv.kanban.lane.{key}') and a list of cards. Lane label: if key=='__untagged__' show 'Untagged', else show key.
9. Each card (author_id='bcv.kanban.card.{block_id}') shows block.title ?? block.block_id. Make cards draggable using egui's DragAndDrop or a manual drag tracker.
10. On drop: get the from_lane_key from KanbanDragState, get the to_lane_key from the drop target lane. Call updateLoomBlock(block_id, { add_tags: to_key=='__untagged__' ? [] : [to_key], remove_tags: from_key=='__untagged__' ? [] : [from_key] }). Set status='Moving card...' while in flight. After call completes, call queryBlockViewResults to re-query. Never mutate local lane arrays — the re-query is the source of truth.
11. Show a busy indicator (status='Moving card...') while drop mutation is in flight. Accept no further drops while busy.

CalendarSubView:
12. Render blocks bucketed by date field (journal_date, created, or updated per definition.calendar_date_field). Use the same bucketKey() logic as React LoomCalendarView: journal_date -> block.journal_date ?? 'undated', updated -> block.updated_at.slice(0,10), default -> block.created_at.slice(0,10).
13. Sort bucket keys alphabetically. Render each bucket as a day column or day strip (author_id='bcv.calendar.day.{date_key}'). Each entry is a row (author_id='bcv.calendar.entry.{block_id}') showing block title.
14. Date-range filtering is backend-side: when the user selects a date range in a date picker widget, update definition.query.date_from and definition.query.date_to, call updateBlockView, then queryBlockViewResults. Render a simple date range input (two egui::DatePickerButton or text field inputs for from/to). Author_ids: 'bcv.calendar.date-from', 'bcv.calendar.date-to'.

New View creation:
15. Show a '+ New view' button (author_id='bcv.new-view'). Opens a small popup with: kind selector, title input, confirm. On confirm, call createBlockView and then switch to the new view_block_id.

AccessKit summary: all interactive elements use stable author_ids as listed; table header buttons, table rows, kanban lanes, kanban cards, calendar day headers, calendar entries, kind switcher buttons, status bar, new-view button.

Dependencies / unblock conditions: MT-021 must be complete (HTTP client). MT-023 must be complete (tag identity used for Kanban lane keys from tag hubs). No dependency on MT-022, MT-024, MT-025, MT-026.

Risk if missed: The block-collection views (table, kanban, calendar) are absent from the native frontend. This is a major knowledge management surface (Notion/Obsidian databases parity).
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Opening a saved table-kind view block loads the definition and results from real PG. The table renders rows for all returned blocks with correct column values (title, updated_at verified).

AC2: Clicking a table header sort button sends updateBlockView with the new sort definition and re-queries; the rows reorder according to the backend sort. Clicking the same header again toggles direction.
AC3: Opening a kanban-kind view block renders lanes from results.groups. Each lane shows its label (or 'Untagged') and its cards.

AC4: Dragging a Kanban card from one lane to another calls updateLoomBlock with the correct add_tags/remove_tags values, then re-queries. The card appears in the new lane after re-query. The original lane no longer contains the card (proved by backend, not local state).
AC5: Opening a calendar-kind view block renders day buckets with block titles correctly bucketed by the definition.calendar_date_field.
AC6: Changing the date range (date_from/date_to) calls updateBlockView and re-queries; the calendar re-renders with the filtered block set.
AC7: The kind switcher changes the view kind (table->kanban->calendar) by calling updateBlockView and re-querying.
AC8: The '+ New view' button creates a new view block via createBlockView and switches to it.

AC9: AccessKit tree contains 'bcv.table.sort.{field}' for each column header, 'bcv.table.row.{block_id}' for each row, 'bcv.kanban.lane.{key}' for each lane, 'bcv.kanban.card.{block_id}' for each card, 'bcv.calendar.day.{date}' for each bucket.
AC10: With a view definition that has no matching blocks, the respective sub-view renders an empty state (no rows / no cards / no calendar days) without panic.
proof_targets: PROOF1: cargo test -p handshake-native loom::block_collection_view -- --nocapture passes unit tests for: (a) flipDirection() asc/desc toggle logic, (b) bucketKey() for all three date field variants, (c) BLOCK_VIEW_UNTAGGED_LANE sentinel handling in add_tags/remove_tags.
PROOF2: kittest table test with 3 seeded blocks in real PG, table-kind view: assert 3 'bcv.table.row.*' AccessKit nodes and column cells for 'title' field are non-empty.
PROOF3: kittest sort test: click 'bcv.table.sort.title' (initial asc), assert updateBlockView API call with sort={field:'title', direction:'asc'}; click again, assert direction='desc'.

PROOF4: kittest kanban card-move test: drag 'bcv.kanban.card.block-001' from 'bcv.kanban.lane.tag-a' to 'bcv.kanban.lane.tag-b', assert updateLoomBlock called with {add_tags:['tag-b'], remove_tags:['tag-a']}, assert 'bcv.kanban.card.block-001' in 'bcv.kanban.lane.tag-b' after re-query.
PROOF5: kittest calendar test with 2 blocks having different journal_dates in real PG: assert 2 'bcv.calendar.day.*' AccessKit nodes, each containing 1 'bcv.calendar.entry.*' node.
PROOF6: kittest new-view test: click 'bcv.new-view', fill title='Test View', select kind=table, click confirm, assert createBlockView API called and view switches to the new view_block_id.
implementation_notes: flipDirection logic (from React BlockCollectionView.tsx lines 39-47): if same field and current direction='asc' -> 'desc'; if same field and direction='desc' -> 'asc'; if new field -> 'asc'.
BLOCK_VIEW_UNTAGGED_LANE sentinel: the string '__untagged__'. When from_key==UNTAGGED, remove_tags=[]. When to_key==UNTAGGED, add_tags=[]. This must match exactly (api.ts line 1142).

bucketKey() (from React LoomCalendarView.tsx lines 16-24): for journal_date field -> block.journal_date ?? 'undated'; for updated -> block.updated_at[0..10]; default -> block.created_at[0..10]. These are ISO date strings.

Kanban drag in egui: use egui::DragAndDrop::set_payload(lane_drag_state) on card drag start; on lane dragover check egui::DragAndDrop::payload::<KanbanDragState>() and render a drop indicator; on frame where payload is claimed (DragAndDrop::take_payload) and pointer released over the lane, fire the mutation.
Do not sort or filter results client-side at any point. All sort, filter, group operations are backend-driven via updateBlockView + queryBlockViewResults.
queryBlockViewResults uses POST (not GET) per api.ts line 1944. The body contains {limit, offset}. Ensure the HTTP client sends POST with JSON body, not query params.
Busy guard for Kanban: set kanban_drag=Some(state) on drag start; set busy=true on drop fire; re-enable drops only after re-query completes. Reject further drop events while busy=true.
Calendar date picker: if egui's DatePickerButton is not available in the egui version in use, fall back to two egui::TextEdit singleline inputs accepting 'YYYY-MM-DD' format with inline validation.

New view popup: use egui::Window with a form containing kind radio buttons and a title TextEdit. On confirm, call createBlockView({kind, title, query:{}}). Switch view_block_id to the new block.block_id from the response.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/BlockCollectionView.tsx — entire host component (primary reference), flipDirection function (lines 39-47), handleSortChange (lines 93-108), handleCardMove (lines 110-127)
app/src/components/LoomTableView.tsx — column labels, cellValue(), sortIndicator(), sort button data-testids
app/src/components/LoomKanbanView.tsx — DRAG_MIME, DragState, handleDrop, BLOCK_VIEW_UNTAGGED_LANE usage
BlockViewDefinition, BlockViewResults, BlockViewLane, BLOCK_VIEW_UNTAGGED_LANE: app/src/lib/api.ts lines 1082-1142
binds_backend_api: PATCH /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks/{block_id} — updateLoomBlock (body: {add_tags, remove_tags}) for Kanban lane move

risks: RISK-1: queryBlockViewResults uses POST but a developer may mistake it for GET, causing the query to fail silently (405) or send params as query string instead of body. Mitigation: add a comment in the source file citing api.ts line 1944; add an integration test that verifies the POST body.

RISK-2: Kanban drag-and-drop in egui may not work if the coder uses a widget that captures the egui drag event before the lane drop target can claim it. Mitigation: use egui::DragAndDrop payload API (not native OS drag); test with PROOF4.

RISK-3: Concurrent sort + card-move operations could fire both updateBlockView and updateLoomBlock simultaneously, causing the re-query to return ambiguous state. Mitigation: use a single in-flight flag; block sort clicks while kanban move is in flight and vice versa.

RISK-4: BLOCK_VIEW_UNTAGGED_LANE sentinel '__untagged__' hardcoded in both frontend and React; if the backend changes this value, both break. Mitigation: define it as a constant in the Rust frontend matching the React constant exactly; document the coupling.

RISK-5: Calendar date picker fallback (text input) may accept invalid date strings that crash the backend filter. Mitigation: validate the date string against regex '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' before including in updateBlockView body; show inline validation error if invalid.

RISK-6: Large result sets (>100 blocks) in table view may cause egui scroll performance issues. Mitigation: use egui::ScrollArea with virtual row rendering (show_rows with row_height) for the table to avoid rendering offscreen rows.
minimum_controls: MC-1: POST body for queryBlockViewResults verified in integration test. Covered by PROOF2.
MC-2: egui::DragAndDrop payload API for Kanban drag. Covered by PROOF4.
MC-3: Single in-flight flag blocking concurrent sort + card-move. Covered by AC4 (card move re-queries correctly).
MC-4: BLOCK_VIEW_UNTAGGED_LANE as a named Rust constant. Covered by PROOF1.
MC-5: Date string regex validation before updateBlockView. Covered by AC6.
MC-6: Virtual row rendering in table ScrollArea for large result sets. Covered by AC1 (performance criterion implied by real PG data test).
